title: Protective Effects of Vitamin D Against Doxorubicin Chemotherapy-Induced Hepatotoxicity in Wistar Albino Rats: Evidence frommTc-Pyrophosphate Scintigraphy and Oxidative-Inflammatory Pathways.
snippet: Doxorubicin, a widely used chemotherapeutic agent, is known to induce hepatotoxicity through oxidative stress and inflammatory pathways. Vitamin D has been reported to exert antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects; however, its potential protective role in doxorubicin-induced liver injury remains insufficiently characterized.Adult male Wistar albino rats w...
